name: Spot (SS-413)
events: 14 Nov 1944 With her trials and initial training completed USS Spot (Cmdr William Schuyler Post, Jr.) arrived at Pearl Harbor.
4 Dec 1944 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) departed from Pearl Harbor for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the East China and Yellow Seas.

10 Dec 1944 Around 2000 L/10, in approximate position 21°00'N, 169°45'E, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) made rendezvous with USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN) and USS Balao (T/Cdr. M.R. de Arellano, USN). Course was set for Saipan. En-route exercises were carried out. (1)
15 Dec 1944 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) arrived at Saipan. She departed from this base for her patrol area two day later.

15 Dec 1944 Around 1020 K/15, USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN), USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Balao (T/Cdr. M.R. de Arellano, USN) arrived at Saipan. They were escorted in by USS PC 1127 (Lt.(jg) G.O. Pratt, USNR) which had joined around 0600 K/15.
At Saipan the submarines were taken in hand for voyage repairs by USS Fulton (T/Capt. A.A. Clarkson, USN). (1)
17 Dec 1944 Around 1400 K/17, USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN), USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) and USS Balao (T/Cdr. M.R. de Arellano, USN) departed Saipan for their patrol area.
USS Razorback (T/Lt.Cdr. C.D. Brown, USN) was also in company until 2030 K/17 when she parted company to proceed independently.
The submarines were escorted out until 2030 K/17 by USS Herald (Lt. E.P. Dietrich, USNR). (1)

21 Dec 1944 At 1530 I/21, while patrolling dived, south of Kyushu, Japan, in position 29°34'N, 131°45'E, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) sighted a surfaced US submarine bearing 018° at a range of 10000 yards heading towards. Changed course to get off his track.
This was USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN) which was not aware that she was sighted by USS Icefish. (1)
7 Jan 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) sank the Japanese guard boat Nichiei Maru No.2 (78 GRT) and two Japanese trawlers with gunfire in the East China Sea in position 31°20'N, 123°40'E.
14 Jan 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) sank two Japanese trawlers with gunfire in the East China Sea in position 29°58'N, 123°02'E.
19 Jan 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) torpedoed and sank the Japanese merchant cargo ship Usa Maru (184 GRT) in the Yellow Sea in position 39°07'N, 122°51'E.
20 Jan 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) sank the Japanese merchant fishing vessel Tokiwa Maru (221 GRT) with gunfire in the Yellow Sea in position 34°45'N, 124°10'E.
30 Jan 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) ended her 1st war patrol at Midway.
24 Feb 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) departed from Midway for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the East China Sea.

12 Mar 1945 At 1243 I/12, in position 28°14'N, 137°25'E, USS Jallao (T/Cdr. J.B. Icenhower, USN) sighted a US submarine on the horizon. Shortly afterwards recognition signals were exchanged by SJ radar with USS Spot (T/Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr., USN) which had sighted USS Jallao at 1230 I/12. (2)

17 Mar 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) torpedoed and sank the Japanese army cargo ship Nanking Maru (3005 GRT) off Yushiyama Island in position 25°28'N, 120°10'E, and torpedoed and damaged the Japanese merchant cargo ship Ikomasan Maru (3173 GRT) which is beached off Matsu Island in position 26°07'N, 119°57'E. The Ikomasan Maru is finally destroyed by USAAF bombers on 26 March 1945.
23 Mar 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. William Schuyler Post, Jr.) arrived at Saipan to take on stores and provisions, and then resumes her 2nd war patrol on the 27th.
4 May 1945 USS Spot (Cdr. W.S. Post, Jr.) ended her 2nd war patrol at Saipan.
2 Jun 1945 USS Spot (Lt.Cdr. Jack Murray Seymour) departed from Saipan for her 3rd war patrol, and was ordered to patrol in the East China and Yellow Seas.
9 Jul 1945 USS Spot (Lt.Cdr. J.M. Seymour) sank two small Japanese vessels with gunfire in the Yellow Sea in position 38°18'N, 124°03'E.
29 Jul 1945 USS Spot (Lt.Cdr. Jack Murray Seymour) ended her 3rd war patrol at Pearl Harbor.
